title: Mike Krzyzewski threw a subtle jab at Bill Belichick in response to new UNC hire
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: UNC hired former Nuggets coach Michael Malone as the men's basketball coach, replacing Hubert Davis.
Former Duke coach Mike Krzyzewski commented on UNC's hiring of two pro-level coaches, referencing Bill Belichick's role.
Belichick's first season coaching UNC football ended with a 4-8 record, following Mack Brown's 6-7 season in 2024.

title: Person stabs dog in Milton during alleged attack
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: State Police are investigating a stabbing of a dog during an alleged attack in Milton on Blue Hill River Road.
An off-leash dog reportedly attacked a person, who then stabbed the dog in self-defense, according to police.
The injured dog was taken to a local veterinary clinic, and Milton Animal Control is conducting an investigation.

title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Trevor Story moved to No. 5 in Red Sox lineup after a slow start to the 2026 season.
Masataka Yoshida now bats second in the lineup, with Roman Anthony as leadoff hitter.
Alex Cora's lineup changes aim to improve Red Sox performance, now 2-7 on the season.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers aim to retain the fourth seed to avoid a second-round playoff matchup with the Boston Celtics.
Cavaliers have not advanced past the second round since the 2018 NBA Finals, with the Celtics defeating them in 2024.
Cleveland is dealing with lineup uncertainties and multiple player injuries as they conclude the regular season.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts residents face varying electricity costs, with municipal utilities often charging half as much as large investor-owned companies.
Eversource earned nearly 16 cents profit per dollar from customers in 2025, compared to the 13-cent national average.
Switching to municipal utilities requires voter approval and significant infrastructure costs, but municipal aggregation is an alternative for 225 communities.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students rallied over lack of transparency after recent violence near campus, urging better communication from university officials.
A fatal police shooting near Northeastern raised campus safety concerns; the university sent alerts stating it occurred off-campus.
Northeastern's communication methods criticized for relying on external updates; students call for broader alerts for on/off-campus incidents.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: BU President Melissa Gilliam pauses signage policy after removal of three Pride flags sparked protests.
Policy prohibits materials, including Pride flags, from being affixed to outward-facing University-owned property.
Gilliam cited plans for further discussion to address impacts on LGBTQ+ students and community support.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade by sending pitcher Patrick Galle to the Cardinals.
Patrick Galle, 22, drafted from Ole Miss, pitched for Single A Salem before the trade.
Sonny Gray, 36, began season 1-0 with a 4.50 ERA in two starts for the Red Sox.

title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’: Episode 2 recap
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Episode 2 of 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' features drama-filled lunches and escalated tensions among housewives.
Jo-Ellen Tiberi faces rumors about her behavior, while Ashley Iaconetti deals with stress at Audrey's Coffee House.
New cast member Rulla Nehme Pontarelli is introduced, stirring tensions with accusations of infidelity involving her husband.

title: Mass. House to vote on youth social media ban, strict school cellphone ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts House to vote on banning social media for children under 14 and requiring parental consent for ages 14-15.
Proposed legislation includes a 'bell-to-bell' cellphone ban in schools, requiring state guidance and a 10-district pilot program.
Quincy schools propose all-day cellphone ban with secured pouches, awaiting full School Committee decision.
